Here's what I found: ### 1. **Overview Page Component Location** **File**: `/Users/marc/conductor/workspaces/rudel/madrid/apps/web/src/pages/dashboard/OverviewPage.tsx` The OverviewPage is the main dashboard landing page (indexed route at `/dashboard`) and serves as the entry point after login. ### 2. **How Overview Page Handles Empty States** The OverviewPage currently **does NOT have explicit empty state handling**. Instead, it: - Shows a **loading state** while KPIs are fetching (lines 59-66): - Displays a centered Spinner with "Loading dashboard data..." - Uses the `Spinner` component from `@/components/ui/spinner` - **Conditionally renders sections based on data availability** (lines 68-175): - Uses `!kpisLoading && kpis` to only render content when data loads - Each chart section (`usageTrendData`, `modelTokensData`, `insights`) has conditional rendering with `&& data.length > 0` - However, there's no fallback if KPIs are zero or no data exists ### 3. **Components Used by Overview Page** The page uses these analytics components: | Component | Purpose | |-----------|---------| | `PageHeader` | Title, description, and action area (date picker) | | `StatCard` | Displays KPI metrics with icons and optional trend/links | | `AnalyticsCard` | Wrapper container (bg-input, rounded, bordered, padded) | | `DatePicker` | Date range selection in header | | `InsightCard` | Small insight messages with severity colors | | `UsageTrendChart` | Multi-metric trend visualization | | `ModelTokensChart` | Token breakdown by model | | `Spinner` | Loading indicator | All these are imported from `/components/analytics/` or `/components/ui/`. ### 4. **Empty State Patterns in Other Pages** I found **two distinct empty state patterns** across the app: #### Pattern A: Simple Conditional Message (ProjectsListPage, lines 238-242) ```tsx {projects && projects.length > 0 ? )} ``` - Minimal, text-only approach - Used when data is empty but page still loaded #### Pattern B: Comprehensive Empty State (ErrorsPage, lines 237-263) ```tsx : (

No recurring errors found!

Great job! Your team hasn't encountered any recurring errors...

) ``` - Icon + heading + description - Used when data is intentionally empty (success state) #### Pattern C: Feature Not Available (LearningsPage, lines 134-162) ```tsx {!isLoading && !hasData && (

No learnings yet

This feature is powered by our internal continuous improvement system at ObsessionDB...

)} ``` - Icon + heading + description + contextual link/explanation - Used when feature is pending/coming soon - More elaborate padding (`py-20`) ### 5. **Key Observations About Empty States** 1. **No universal empty state component** - patterns are inline in each page 2. **Loading states are consistent** - all pages use `Spinner` in a centered div with "Loading..." message 3. **Empty data vs. loading** - most pages check `isLoading` before showing empty messages 4. **Conditional sections** - charts/tables only render if they have data (`.length > 0`) 5. **Overview page gap** - The overview page doesn't show an empty state if KPIs return 0 values; it would just show empty stat cards 6. **Success messaging** - When no errors exist, ErrorsPage shows a celebratory empty state with checkmark icon 7. **Information-rich empty states** - LearningsPage provides context about why data is empty and links to relevant info ### File Structure Summary ``` apps/web/src/ ├── pages/dashboard/ │ ├── OverviewPage.tsx (Main dashboard - no empty state) │ ├── SessionsListPage.tsx (Has table with conditional empty row count) │ ├── ProjectsListPage.tsx (Pattern A: Simple message) │ ├── DevelopersListPage.tsx (No explicit empty state) │ ├── ErrorsPage.tsx (Pattern B: Success icon + message) │ ├── LearningsPage.tsx (Pattern C: Feature placeholder) │ └── ROIPage.tsx (Loading state with spinner) ├── components/analytics/ │ ├── PageHeader.tsx (Title + description + actions) │ ├── AnalyticsCard.tsx (Wrapper - bg-input, rounded, bordered) │ ├── StatCard.tsx (KPI display with icon/trend) │ ├── InsightCard.tsx (Insight messages) │ ├── DatePicker.tsx │ └── ... └── layouts/ └── DashboardLayout.tsx (Sidebar + outlet structure) ```

# rudel

CLI for uploading [Claude Code](https://docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/claude-code) session transcripts to [Rudel](https://app.rudel.ai) for analytics.

## Installation

```bash
npm install -g rudel
```

## Quick Start

```bash
# 1. Log in via your browser
rudel login

# 2. Enable automatic session uploads
rudel enable

# That's it! Your Claude Code sessions will now be uploaded automatically.
```

## Commands

### `rudel login`

Authenticate with Rudel. Opens your browser to [app.rudel.ai](https://app.rudel.ai) where you sign in, then the CLI receives a token automatically.

### `rudel enable`

Registers a [Claude Code hook](https://docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/claude-code/hooks) that automatically uploads your session transcript when a Claude Code session ends. This is the recommended way to use Rudel -- set it and forget it.

### `rudel disable`

Removes the auto-upload hook.

### `rudel upload [session]`

Upload session transcripts. Run without arguments to interactively select projects for batch upload, or pass a session ID / file path to upload a single session.

```bash
# Interactive project picker (batch upload)
rudel upload

# Upload by session ID
rudel upload abc123

# Upload a specific file
rudel upload ./path/to/session.jsonl

# Preview without uploading
rudel upload --dry-run

# Auto-classify sessions
rudel upload --classify
```

When run without arguments, `rudel upload` scans `~/.claude/projects/` for all projects with session transcripts and presents an interactive picker. The current project (matched from your working directory) and its subfolders are pre-selected. Use arrow keys to navigate, space to toggle, and enter to confirm.

### `rudel whoami`

Show the currently authenticated user.

### `rudel logout`

Clear stored credentials.

## Links

- **Web App**: [app.rudel.ai](https://app.rudel.ai)
- **Issues**: [GitHub Issues](https://github.com/obsessiondb/rudel/issues)

# PR Creation Checklist

Follow these steps in order when creating a pull request. Do not skip any step.

## 1. Run Verification

```bash
bun run verify
```

## 2. Review the Workspace Diff

Use `mcp__conductor__GetWorkspaceDiff` with `stat: true` to see all changed files, then review the full diff to understand the scope of changes.

## 3. Commit All Changes

Stage and commit all changes. Follow any user instructions about commit message style.

## 4. Push and Create PR

1. Push to origin with `-u` flag if the branch has no upstream
2. PR title **must** use conventional commit format (enforced by CI):
   `feat:` | `fix:` | `docs:` | `style:` | `refactor:` | `perf:` | `test:` | `build:` | `ci:` | `chore:` | `revert:`

   - Title under 80 characters

```bash
## Summary
- bullet points describing changes

## Test plan
- [ ] testing steps

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
EOF
)"
```

## Common Mistakes to Avoid

- **Skipping verification**: Step 1 is non-negotiable. CI will catch the same failures but slower.
- **Wrong PR title format**: Must be conventional commit format or CI will reject it.
- **Describing only the latest commit**: The PR description should cover the entire branch diff.
